title: The Sewage Capacity Gap: What Our Plants Can’t Handle
caption: Even in a “typical” major city, only about one‑third of daily sewage is actually treated, leaving most wastewater to flow directly into rivers and nallas.

items.label: 3,500 MLD
items.description: Total wastewater produced daily by the city's population.

items.label: 1,500 MLD
items.description: Maximum capacity if all treatment plants operate at 100% efficiency.

items.label: 1,200 MLD
items.description: The real amount treated due to broken pipes, power cuts, and maintenance.
